description: Creates a named numeric variable from a shape property and displays its value (e.g., 'a = 3.50'). Updates automatically when the referenced shape changes.
utility: Extract a computed property from a shape and store it as a named variable

arguments.name: position
arguments.description: Grid position for the KaTeX display of variable = value

arguments.name: shapeRef
arguments.description: Reference to the shape whose property is being extracted (for dependency tracking)

arguments.name: propertyExpr
arguments.description: Property expression string using p() syntax, e.g., "p(point_2, x)" or "p(line_1, distance)"

arguments.name: variableName
arguments.description: Name of the variable to register with the computed value (e.g., "a", "d")

arguments.name: styles
arguments.description: Optional styling: c(), fc(), fo(), fs(), etc.

alternates.variant: Extract x coordinate of a point
alternates.description: Variable 'a' holds the x-coordinate of point_2, updates dynamically
Variable 'd' holds the distance (length) of line_1

alternates.syntax: cprop_3 = computedproperty(at(4,10), circle_1, "p(circle_1, area)", "A", c(blue), fs(16))

examples.scenario: Track x-coordinate of a point as variable
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(5,9), 20, 20, type(plotting))
P = point(G, 3, 4)
cprop_1 = computedproperty(at(2,10), P, "p(P, x)", "a")

examples.scenario: Track distance of a line segment
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(5,9), 20, 20)
A = point(G, 0, 0)
B = point(G, 3, 4)
L = line(G, A, B, type(segment))
cprop_1 = computedproperty(at(2,10), L, "p(L, distance)", "d")

examples.scenario: Use computed variable in subsequent expressions
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(5,9), 20, 20)
P = point(G, 3, 4)
cprop_1 = computedproperty(at(2,10), P, "p(P, x)", "px")
cprop_2 = computedproperty(at(3,10), P, "p(P, y)", "py")
desc(at(4,10), "Distance from origin = :{sqrt(px^2 + py^2)}")

notes: The variable updates dynamically when the referenced shape moves or changes
Displayed as 'variableName = value' using KaTeX rendering
Similar to desc() for evaluation but also registers a usable variable like variableanimator()
The property expression string uses p() syntax: p(shapeRef, propertyName)
